solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int findIntegers(int n) {
        int dp[32] = {0};
        dp[0] = 1;
        dp[1] = 2;
        for (int i = 2; i < 31; ++i) {
            dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2];
        }

        int result = 0;
        int prev_bit = 0;
        for (int i = 29; i >= 0; --i) {
            int mask = 1 << i;
            int bit = (n & mask) >> i;
            if (bit == 1) {
                result += dp[i];
                if (prev_bit == 1) {
                    return result;
                }
                prev_bit = 1;
            } else {
                prev_bit = 0;
            }
        }

        return result + 1;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses dynamic programming to precompute the number of valid binary numbers of length i without consecutive ones. It then iterates through the bits of n, using the precomputed values to count valid numbers less than or equal to n. The key strength is the efficient use of DP and bit manipulation, while the weakness is the limited applicability of the approach to similar problems.
